scope_boundary: Public-safe local external Circle seed-rule exact-regeneration receipt import only. The slice records one pinned `CC-AI-CONTRACT-SEED-RULE-001` structural fixture at Circle commit `63b0f511`, `fixture_n=128`, `exact_regeneration=true`, `generator_length=383`, `explicit_length=454`, `storage_saving=71`, `bounded_search_candidate_count=3`, `bounded_search_exact_candidate_count=2`, 32 theorem IDs, two planner recommendations, contract fingerprint `836594a5f1d448900797e595cb98f0e476c0b9cbd7365fe333cf7ae2622f13c5`, command-output digests, and passing targeted Circle CLI test summaries. It does not prove useful compression, codec correctness, semantic utility, useful generation, deployed generator behavior, optimal search, model quality, runtime, memory scaling, transfer, safety, ASI, or Compact Generative Systems chapter-core promotion.
transition_reason: The seed-rule receipt slice makes one Circle exact-regeneration and storage-accounting fixture concrete in the Compact Generative Systems chapter and closes a narrow missing-evidence-surface blocker for compact seeds. The evidence remains an external-project finite fixture with no deployed generator, no codec benchmark, no downstream utility measurement, no arbitrary-artifact reconstruction test, no semantic-utility test, no fallback execution, no optimal-search proof, and no independent review, so it blocks promotion rather than moving support state.
